key_insight: AI is positioned as a core enabler for the SKA era, turning extreme data volume and complexity into opportunities for automated analysis, control, and discovery.
engineering_relevance: The work highlights AI methods that can scale radio astronomy workflows in real time, supporting source detection, interference mitigation, calibration, imaging, and autonomous operations.
method: Deep learning for source detection, anomaly detection, and parameter inference
Generative and self-supervised models for sky simulation, calibration, and imaging
Reinforcement learning and federated learning for scheduling, control, and distributed learning
evidence_signal: The abstract frames the challenge through petabyte-scale datasets and terabit-per-second data streams, motivating AI across real-time and offline SKAO workflows.
watch_item: Scientific deployment will require explainability, uncertainty quantification, and physics-informed biases to avoid opaque or physically inconsistent results.
paper_title: The Role of Artificial Intelligence in the SKA Era
